135.question: How would you apply the order of operations to simplify a mathematical expression?

answer1.text:
To simplify a math expression, use these steps:

1. First, do the calculations inside parentheses or brackets.
2. Next, solve any exponents or roots.
3. Then, do all multiplication and division from left to right.
4. Finally, do all addition and subtraction from left to right.

This order is called PEMDAS. It stands for Parentheses, Exponents, Multiplication/Division, Addition/Subtraction. If there is more than one multiplication or division, or more than one addition or subtraction, work from left to right.

For example, in the expression 3 plus 6 times (5 plus 4) divided by 3 squared, minus 7, first do the parentheses, then the exponents, then multiplication and division, then addition and subtraction.

If you always follow these steps, you will simplify math expressions the correct way every time.

answer2.text:
To simplify a mathematical expression using the order of operations, follow these steps:

1. First, solve anything inside parentheses or grouping symbols.
2. Next, evaluate exponents or roots.
3. Then, perform all multiplications before any divisions in the expression.
4. Lastly, complete all additions and subtractions from left to right.

This order is often remembered by the acronym PEMDAS: Parentheses, Exponents, Multiplication/Division, Addition/Subtraction. If an expression has multiple operations of the same rank, work from left to right. For example, in the expression 3 plus 6 times (5 plus 4) divided by 3 squared, minus 7, first handle the parentheses, then exponents, then multiplication and division, then addition and subtraction. By consistently applying this order, you ensure that expressions are simplified correctly every time.

136.question: How could you use your knowledge of the human body to explain why you get tired after physical exercise?

answer1.text: After physical exercise, the body gets tired due to several reasons. Muscles use energy in the form of ATP when they contract. During intense activity, ATP is used faster than it can be produced, causing fatigue. Oxygen is needed to make ATP efficiently, but during hard exercise, oxygen delivery cannot keep up with demand, leading to anaerobic respiration. This produces glucose, which builds up in muscles and causes tiredness. Exercise also leads to the loss of fluids and minerals in sweat, resulting in dehydration and imbalance of salts, which can make you feel weak. Nervous system fatigue plays a role too, as repeated signals from nerves become less effective. Additionally, the heart and lungs work harder to supply oxygen and remove carbon dioxide, increasing overall energy use. All these processes combined make you feel tired after exercise and signal your body to rest and recover.
answer1.label: gpt-4.1_errors

answer2.text: After exercise, your body feels tired for several reasons. When your muscles work, they use energy called ATP. During hard exercise, your body uses ATP faster than it can make more, so you feel tired. To make ATP well, your body needs oxygen. But during heavy exercise, your body cannot bring enough oxygen to your muscles, so your muscles use a process without oxygen. This makes lactic acid, which collects in your muscles and makes you feel tired. Exercise also makes you sweat, so you lose water and important minerals. This can cause dehydration and an imbalance of salts in your body, making you feel weak. Also, your nerves get tired when they must send a lot of signals to your muscles. Your heart and lungs have to work harder to give your body more oxygen and take away carbon dioxide, so your body uses more energy. All these things together make you feel tired after exercise, and tell your body that it should rest and recover.
answer2.label: gpt-4.1

137.question: How do you apply the principles of supply and demand to explain why the price of an item might go up?

answer1.text: The principles of supply and demand state that the price of an item is influenced by how much of it is available and how much people want it. When demand for an item increases but the supply stays the same, people are willing to pay more, so the price goes up. On the other hand, if the supply of an item decreases and demand stays steady, there is less available for buyers, causing competition and higher prices. For example, if a popular new phone is released but plenty of units are made, many people want it but few can get it, so the price rises. Similarly, if a crop fails and less food is produced, but people still need the same amount of food, prices also increase. Thus, prices usually go up when there is higher demand or lower supply.
answer1.label: gpt-4.1_errors

answer2.text: The rules of supply and demand say that the price of something depends on how much of it there is and how many people want it. If more people want something but the amount available does not change, they will pay more, so the price increases. If there is less of something for sale but people still want it, there is not enough for everyone, and the price also goes up. For example, if a new and popular phone comes out but only a few are made, many people will want it, but only a few can buy it, so the price goes up. Another example is if a crop is damaged and there is less food, but people still need the same amount, food prices go up too. So, prices usually rise when more people want something or when there is less of it.

138.question: Using the laws of motion, how would you explain why seat belts are necessary in cars?

answer1.text: Seat belts are necessary in cars due to the first law of motion, also called the law of inertia. This law states that an object in motion stays in motion, and an object at rest stays at rest, unless acted on by an external force. When a car suddenly stops due to a collision, the car’s body stops, but the passengers inside continue moving at the same speed the car was traveling. Without a seat belt, this can cause passengers to collide with the interior of the car, leading to injury. A seat belt provides the necessary internal force to stop the passenger’s motion together with the car, preventing them from being thrown forward. Therefore, seat belts are necessary to keep passengers safe by reducing the risk of injury, in accordance with the laws of motion.
answer1.label: gpt-4.1_errors

answer2.text: Seat belts are important in cars because of the first law of motion, also called the law of inertia. This law says that something moving will keep moving, and something still will stay still, unless something else makes it change. When a car stops suddenly in an accident, the car itself stops, but the people inside keep moving at the same speed as before. If you are not wearing a seat belt, you can hit parts of the car inside, which can hurt you. A seat belt gives the force that makes your body stop with the car, so you do not fly forward. This is why seat belts are important—they help keep you safe and lower the chance of getting hurt, according to how motion works.
answer2.label: gpt-4.1

139.question: How would you use your knowledge of nutrition to plan a well-balanced meal?

answer1.text: To plan a well-balanced meal, first include a variety of food groups: lean protein, whole grains, vegetables, fruits, and a small amount of healthy fat. For example, choose grilled chicken or beans for protein, brown rice or whole wheat bread for grains, and a colorful mix of vegetables such as spinach and carrots. Add a serving of fruit, like an apple or berries, for vitamins and fiber. Include a small portion of healthy fat, such as olive oil dressing or a few nuts. Aim for half the plate to be protein, one quarter vegetables and fruit, and one quarter grains. Limit processed foods, excess salt, and added sugars. Drink water instead of sugary beverages. This approach ensures a balance of macronutrients (protein, carbohydrates, fat), ample vitamins and minerals, and supports overall health.
answer1.label: gpt-4.1_errors

answer2.text: To make a healthy meal, include different types of foods: lean protein, whole grains, vegetables, fruits, and a small amount of healthy fat. Add a fruit, such as an apple or some berries, for more vitamins and fiber. Include a little healthy fat, like olive oil on your salad or a few nuts. Try to fill half of your plate with vegetables and fruit, one quarter with protein, and one quarter with grains. Try not to eat too many processed foods, too much salt, or added sugar. Drink water instead of sweet drinks. This way of eating gives you a good balance of protein, carbohydrates, and fat, along with enough vitamins and minerals, and helps you stay healthy.

140.question: If you were a pharmacist, how would you determine the expiration dates of various medicines?

answer1.text: As a pharmacist, I would determine the expiration dates of medicines by checking the manufacturer’s packaging for the printed expiration date, which is based on stability studies ensuring potency and safety up to that date. For medicines that are repackaged or dispensed in smaller containers, I would use the manufacturer’s original expiration date or assign a beyond-use date (BUD) according to guidelines, usually found in the United States Pharmacopeia (USP). The BUD often depends on the dosage form, storage conditions, and whether the package has been opened. I would also consider storage recommendations, as improper storage does not affect the effective shelf life. 141.question: Given your understanding of condensed water cycle, how would you use it to explain why a glass filled with a cold drink gets wet on the outside on a hot day?

answer1.text: When a glass is filled with a cold drink on a hot day, the temperature of the glass surface rises above the dew point of the surrounding air. The air contains water vapor, which is an invisible gaseous form of water. When this moist air touches the cold surface of the glass, it cools down rapidly. As air cools, it cannot hold as much water vapor, and the excess water vapor condenses into liquid droplets on the outside of the glass. This process is called condensation, which is a key part of the condensed water cycle. The cycle involves water evaporating into vapor and then condensing back into liquid. So, the wetness on the glass comes from water in the air, not from the drink inside the glass. This demonstrates how cooling causes condensation, which is a fundamental aspect of the water cycle.
answer1.label: gpt-4.1_errors

answer2.text: When you pour a cold drink into a glass on a hot day, the outside of the glass becomes colder than the air around it. The air has water vapor in it, which means tiny water particles that you cannot see. When the warm, moist air touches the cold glass, the air gets colder very quickly. Cold air cannot hold as much water vapor as warm air. So, the extra water in the air turns into small drops of water on the outside of the glass. This is called condensation. Condensation is an important part of the water cycle. In the water cycle, water becomes vapor when it is hot, and changes back into liquid when it cools down. So, the water drops on the outside of the glass come from the air, not from the drink inside. This shows that cooling makes water vapor turn into liquid, which is a basic part of how water moves in nature.
answer2.label: gpt-4.1
